Description: Year-over-year change in the ratio of long-term debt to equity, reflecting trends in long-term leverage
Current assets divided by current liabilities, measuring short-term liquidity
Ratio of total debt to total capital (total capital = total debt + equity), representing how much of the company’s capital comes from debt
Ratio of total debt to total equity, a standard leverage metric gauging company indebtedness relative to its equity base
Total debt divided by the sum of market capitalization plus total debt, measuring debt as a proportion of total firm capitalization
Ratio of long-term debt to total capital (total capital = long-term debt + equity), indicating the proportion of the company's capital structure comprised of long-term debt
Ratio of long-term debt to common equity, measuring leverage through long-duration borrowings relative to shareholders’ equity
Operating cash flow divided by current liabilities, indicating the company’s ability to cover short-term obligations with operating cash flow
(Current assets minus inventories) divided by current liabilities, indicating the company’s ability to meet short-term obligations without selling inventory
Market capitalization, the total equity market value of a company’s outstanding shares (in USD)
Altman’s Z-score, an academic formula for predicting company insolvency risk or likelihood of bankruptcy
Probability of default according to the Campbell-Hilscher-Szilagyi (CHS) academic model, a statistical model for forecasting bankruptcy
Distance to default derived from the Merton structural credit risk model, estimating how far the firm is from default based on its asset value, volatility, and liabilities
Ohlson O-score, a statistical model-based indicator estimating probability of bankruptcy
Weighted average time to maturity of all debt instruments outstanding, measuring overall debt duration in years
Weighted average interest rate across all outstanding debt, representing aggregate cost of debt financing
Proprietary measure of the firm’s “distance” or cushion from the insolvency boundary over a 1-year horizon; higher values indicate greater safety from default
Model-estimated probability or risk score of a firm becoming insolvent in the next 1 year under extreme stress scenario, based on capital structure signals
Model-estimated probability or risk score of a firm becoming insolvent in the next 9 months under an extreme stress scenario, based on capital structure characteristics
Year-over-year growth rate in short-term debt maturing within 5 years, indicating changes in near-term debt exposure
Model-measured projection of insolvency risk (probability or risk score) over the next 1 year based on a firm’s capital structure
Model-measured projection of insolvency risk (probability or risk score) over the next 2 years based on a firm’s capital structure
Model-measured projection of insolvency risk (probability or risk score) over the next 9 months based on a firm’s capital structure
Model-estimated probability or risk score of a firm becoming insolvent in the next 1 year under moderate stress scenario, based on capital structure
Model-estimated probability or risk score of a firm becoming insolvent in the next 2 years under a moderate stress scenario, using proprietary capital structure analytics
Model-projected moderate scenario insolvency risk over the next 9 months, factoring in a moderately stressed capital structure scenario
Ratio of cash or available borrowing capacity on revolving credit facilities to total debt, representing additional liquidity buffer
Short Term Debt to Debt Due Within 5 years
Ratio of short-term debt (debt maturing within 1 year) to total debt, indicating the proportion of debt coming due soon
Expected return “alpha” forecast generated by the Cassie composite credit risk model, representing proprietary signal of future stock outperformance or underperformance
Preliminary or early-stage version of the Cassie composite risk score, integrating standard and proprietary credit risk metrics

Composite credit risk or solvency risk score produced by the Cassie model, integrating traditional and alternative risk signals to reflect overall credit quality; higher scores indicate lower insolvency risk
